ai_ml.name: Red Hat OpenShift AI
ai_ml.description: End-to-end MLOps platform for training, serving, and monitoring AI/ML models on OpenShift. Includes model serving with vLLM, KServe, and integrated Jupyter notebooks.
ai_ml.when_to_use: Any deployment requiring model training, fine-tuning, or LLM serving at scale. Supports Intel Gaudi accelerators natively.

ai_ml.name: Node Feature Discovery
ai_ml.description: Detects and advertises hardware features (Intel AMX, AVX-512, Gaudi accelerators) as node labels for intelligent scheduling.
ai_ml.when_to_use: Heterogeneous clusters with mixed hardware — ensures AI workloads land on nodes with the right accelerators.

ai_ml.name: Intel Gaudi Device Plugin
ai_ml.description: Kubernetes device plugin that exposes Intel Gaudi accelerators as schedulable resources in OpenShift.
ai_ml.when_to_use: Clusters with Intel Gaudi accelerators for LLM inference or training workloads.

networking.name: Red Hat Service Mesh
networking.description: Istio-based service mesh for microservice traffic management, mTLS, observability, and canary deployments.
networking.when_to_use: Multi-service architectures needing mutual TLS, traffic shaping, or A/B routing between model versions.

networking.name: MetalLB
networking.description: Bare-metal load balancer for on-premises OpenShift deployments without cloud provider LB integration.
networking.when_to_use: On-prem or edge deployments on Intel hardware that need external service exposure without a cloud load balancer.

storage.name: OpenShift Data Foundation
storage.description: Software-defined storage providing block, file, and object storage backed by Ceph, integrated with OpenShift.

resource_requirements.storage: 3 nodes minimum, 2Ti raw per node recommended

storage.when_to_use: Persistent storage for model weights, training datasets, and database workloads. Required for multi-node model serving.

storage.name: LVM Storage
storage.description: Lightweight local storage provisioner using LVM for single-node or edge deployments.
storage.when_to_use: Single-node OpenShift (SNO) or edge deployments where ODF is too heavy. Good for Intel-powered edge inference nodes.

security.name: Red Hat Advanced Cluster Security
security.description: Kubernetes-native security platform for vulnerability management, compliance, network segmentation, and runtime threat detection.
security.when_to_use: Regulated industries (financial services, healthcare) requiring continuous compliance scanning and runtime protection.

security.name: Trusted Artifact Signer
security.description: Sigstore-based artifact signing for container images and build attestations, ensuring software supply chain integrity.
security.when_to_use: Supply chain security requirements — sign and verify container images, SBOMs, and model artifacts.

security.name: Trustee (Intel TDX Attestation)
security.description: Key Broker Service for Intel TDX confidential computing. Verifies TD quotes and releases secrets only to attested workloads.
security.when_to_use: Confidential AI workloads on Intel TDX-enabled Xeon processors. Protects model weights and inference data in hardware-encrypted enclaves.

virtualization.name: OpenShift Virtualization
virtualization.description: Run and manage virtual machines alongside containers on OpenShift using KubeVirt. Supports live migration, snapshots, and Intel-optimized VM templates.
virtualization.when_to_use: Migrating legacy VM workloads to OpenShift, or running software that requires a full OS environment alongside cloud-native AI services.

edge.name: MicroShift
edge.description: Lightweight OpenShift for edge devices. Runs a single-node Kubernetes with OpenShift APIs on resource-constrained Intel hardware.
edge.when_to_use: Far-edge inference on Intel Xeon or Core Ultra processors. Retail stores, factory floors, remote healthcare clinics.

edge.name: Red Hat Advanced Cluster Management
edge.description: Multi-cluster lifecycle management — deploy, configure, and govern OpenShift clusters at scale across data centers and edge sites.
edge.when_to_use: Managing fleets of OpenShift clusters across hybrid and edge environments. Policy-based governance for Intel hardware standardization.

edge.name: Red Hat OpenShift Pipelines
edge.description: Tekton-based CI/CD pipelines natively integrated with OpenShift for building, testing, and deploying applications.
edge.when_to_use: Automated build and deploy pipelines for AI model serving containers, especially multi-architecture builds targeting Intel platforms.

edge.name: Red Hat OpenShift Serverless
edge.description: Knative-based serverless workloads on OpenShift. Scale-to-zero for intermittent AI inference endpoints.
edge.when_to_use: Bursty or intermittent inference workloads where scale-to-zero saves compute cost on Intel infrastructure.
